# §18.4 Paper-Comparison Table — MOALF-UAV-MEC reproduction

**Generated by:** `python experiments/reproduce_table.py` (one-command runner, §18.4).
**Protocol:** frozen real config, all signed-off values, V frozen; 30-run protocol (seeds 42–71); **nothing tuned toward any paper number (§18).**
**Baselines (stated):** throughput/completion improvement = MOALF vs a **random** offload policy; route reduction = MOALF total UAV travel vs a **straight-flight-to-device-centroid** stand-in. (The paper's own baselines are other algorithms — NSGA-II, MA-DRL, etc. — out of scope; these are clearly-labelled stand-ins.)

## PRIMARY — frozen real config (λ=0.2), 30 runs

| metric | paper-claimed | reproduced (mean±sd) | abs gap | rel gap | tier |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Task completion rate (%) | 94.5 | **98.4 ± 0.5** | +3.9 | +4.1% | **1 (exact)** |
| Throughput increase vs random (%) | 55.0 | **0.6 ± 5.3** | −54.4 | −99% | **3 (divergent)** |
| UAV route reduction (%) | 38.0 | **−11.9 ± 15.3** | −49.9 | −131% | **3 (divergent)** |

MOALF absolutes (context, no paper target): mean latency 0.86 s · utilization 0.31 · energy/task 2007 J · coverage 91.8 · (random-offload completion 98.0%).

## CONTEXT ONLY — high load (λ=0.6), 10 runs — **NOT the paper config, NOT the reproduction**

| metric | paper-claimed | reproduced (mean±sd) | abs gap | rel gap | tier |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Task completion rate (%) | 94.5 | 90.7 ± 9.1 | −3.8 | −4.0% | 1 (exact) |
| Throughput increase vs random (%) | 55.0 | **16.7 ± 6.9** | −38.3 | −70% | **2 (directional)** |
| UAV route reduction (%) | 38.0 | −6.8 ± 7.7 | −44.8 | −118% | 3 (divergent) |

(high load: random-offload completion drops to 71.0%, util 0.92, latency 2.77 s)

## Per-metric findings

- **Task completion (94.5%) → REPRODUCED, Tier 1.** The consistent system reaches **98.4%**, even *exceeding* the claim. Directly consistent with the light-load finding (entries 32/39): demand ≈ ⅓ of capacity, queues empty ~99% of the time, so nearly all tasks complete on time. This is the one headline that reproduces cleanly.

- **Throughput increase (55%) → NOT reproduced at the frozen config (Tier 3), but the mechanism is real (Tier 2 under load).** At λ=0.2 MOALF gives only **+0.6%** over random because the system is underloaded — random offload already completes **98.0%**, leaving essentially nothing to improve (entries 32/39). At λ=0.6 (high load), where random completion falls to 71%, MOALF shows **+16.7%** (Tier 2). So MOALF's throughput advantage is genuine but only **emerges under load**; the paper's stated parameters do not stress the system enough to exhibit a 55% gain.

- **UAV route reduction (38%) → NOT reproduced (Tier 3).** MOALF actually travels **~12% more** than a straight flight to the device centroid. The coverage-driven MPC (entry 43) continuously micro-adjusts toward coverage-optimal positions rather than minimizing travel, and saturated cell-wide SNR (entry 24) gives UAV position **weak leverage**, so there is no pressure to shorten routes. (The route baseline is also only a stand-in; the paper compares against other trajectory optimizers.) The 38% route reduction is not reproducible in this consistent, lightly-loaded, saturated-SNR regime.

## Overall honest assessment (§18)

**One of three headline numbers (completion) reproduces cleanly (Tier 1, even exceeding the claim). The two *relative-improvement* claims (throughput +55%, route −38%) do not reproduce at the frozen parameters (Tier 3)** — and this is **explained, not a bare miss**: the frozen real config is a **lightly-loaded, saturated-SNR regime** (entries 24/32/39) in which (a) baselines already perform near-ceiling so there is little throughput to gain, and (b) trajectory has weak leverage so routes are not minimized. The throughput mechanism **does** work where the system is actually stressed (high-load context, Tier 2).

This is consistent with the §18.1/Tier-3 anticipation: the paper's original relative-improvement numbers likely arose from a **more-stressed regime and/or its specific baseline algorithms**, not from the stated parameters as a consistent implementation runs them. **Manuscript implication:** report completion as reproduced; report the operating load honestly (lightly loaded); and either re-derive the throughput/route improvements against the paper's actual baselines under a stated higher load, or temper those headline figures. **Nothing was tuned to close any gap.**
